Experience with US Laws and Regulations
Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment

scope_requirements.Geography: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Aerospace and industrial manufacturing (85-90% industrial, 10-15% military/defense)

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Katie has exposure to aerospace and defense regulations but her experience depth in this sector requires careful evaluation. She stated her current Industrial Group portfolio at Moog is "probably 90 to 90 being industrial, 10 being defense or even 85," meaning 85-90% of her work involves industrial applications (power generation, paper mills, wood mills) with only 10-15% military/defense work. While she confirmed "I am familiar with the ITAR and, you know, getting ITAR licenses and those kind of

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Katie demonstrates strong cross-functional collaboration and global influence capabilities. She described being "the key contact for the US reg" across 30+ international industrial subsidiaries at Moog, working through "trade compliance champions in each of the foreign entities" who are typically contract managers with part-time compliance duties. She stated she works with "C-suite or, you know, the executive team, that's also something that I'm doing today and, you know, building those relation

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Katie possesses deep hands-on US export compliance expertise across the full regulatory spectrum. She confirmed proficiency in "ITAR, the EAR, the OFAC" and stated that responsibilities including "classifications, licensing, SAP or DECCS. That's all things that I do on a day-to-day basis." She serves as "SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) Business Process Owner" and was "co-business process owner for that project" during the multi-year SAP GTS implementation at Moog. She holds Empowered Official de

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Katie demonstrates acute familiarity with the US export control regulatory environment. She confirmed expertise across "the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) and Office of Foreign Assets Control Regulations (OFAC)" and described acting "as a focal point and strategic advisor on export matters, providing accurate and consistent interpretation of export regulations." She handles "export classification tasks and obtain a Commodity Jurisdiction

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Katie demonstrates comfort and capability in lean, entrepreneurial environments requiring independent operation. She acknowledged Doncasters' lean structure by stating "I get that because like a lot of companies do that. You know, they want to see what they can get done with just one person or a few," indicating familiarity with resource-constrained operations. Her current role is individual contributor ("Industrial, we've always been a team of two—trade compliance manager and then one underneat

compensation.expected_total: $150,000 base salary. Katie previously mentioned $140K via LinkedIn but clarified in interview that she would be "more apt to leave at least if we were closer to like 150" given she is not actively looking and values current company culture
compensation.flexibility: Katie demonstrated flexibility by revising expectations upward from previously mentioned $140K to preferred $150K base, acknowledging this reflects her passive candidate status and satisfaction with current culture. Recruiter confirmed Doncasters can "realistically do a base of 150 plus probably about a 10% bonus" which Katie acknowledged positively

our_take.text: Katie brings solid hands-on US export compliance expertise and has successfully built international compliance programs in a lean environment, which maps well to the Doncasters structure and UK/Germany build-out objective. However, her 85-90% industrial vs. 10-15% defense experience mix is inverted from what Doncasters needs in a primarily aerospace role, and we should validate whether her ITAR classification depth extends beyond the lower-complexity industrial applications that appear to dominate her current portfolio.

candidates.flash_summary: Katie Connacher is Trade Compliance Manager at Moog Inc., based in Buffalo, New York, where she has spent her entire 12+ year professional career within the Industrial Group.

Aerospace and defense contractor (Raytheon — electronics and infrared technologies)

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Mary demonstrates deep aerospace and defense industry expertise spanning 15+ years across multiple major defense contractors. At Raytheon, she manages compliance for electronics and infrared technologies under both ITAR and EAR regulations, with the majority being ITAR-controlled defense work. Her experience at Elbit Systems of America involved managing export compliance for a US-incorporated Israeli-owned defense contractor under FOCI, requiring enhanced government oversight and foreign militar

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Mary demonstrates strong cross-functional influence capabilities, stating she works "with senior leadership across regions" and has "trained and influenced people how to get stuff in and out of the United States" both for external partners and internal teams. At Raytheon, she provides compliance training and education across global operations, working daily with stakeholders to make compliance decisions. She managed multi-site operations at Open Systems (Fort Worth and Florida) and DRS, requirin

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Mary possesses deep US export compliance expertise accumulated over 15 years. She stated she manages "all the licensing" including "writing the licenses, managing all the export, managing all the programs" and has done "classification under the EAR and HTS Schedule B." At Raytheon, she handles ITAR and Commerce licensing, classifications, managing exports using licenses, exemptions, and exceptions, and serves as "Part 130 lead." She managed licensing processes "start to finish" including approva

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Mary demonstrates acute familiarity with the US export control regulatory environment. She explicitly mentioned expertise in "ITAR, EAR compliance" and works daily with these regulations at Raytheon. She referenced DDTC processes, Part 130 leadership, BIS regulations, ECCN classifications, and HTS Schedule B classifications. At Raytheon, she operates under heightened government monitoring due to company compliance issues, stating "the government right now is monitoring them" and "when the govern

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Mary explicitly stated "I like being hands-on" when presented with the individual contributor nature of the Doncasters role. She has worked remotely from home for 9 years at Raytheon, demonstrating comfort operating independently without on-site infrastructure. She manages 12 concurrent programs as an individual contributor, requiring autonomous decision-making and prioritization. Her experience at Elbit under FOCI and at Raytheon under government monitoring suggests comfort with high-stakes, fa

compensation.expected_total: $125,000 base salary (stated as "I would love to go to 125")
compensation.flexibility: Mary stated a clear target of $125,000 base salary representing approximately 15% increase from current $109,000 base. She expressed this as an aspiration ("would love to") rather than a firm requirement, suggesting some flexibility. The Doncasters range of $125,000-$140,000 base plus up to 20% bonus aligns well with her expectations and would represent meaningful total compensation increase from her current approximately $116,000 total cash.

our_take.text: Strong technical fit with deep ITAR/EAR expertise, though entire career has been within large defense contractors rather than mid-sized manufacturing environments.

candidates.flash_summary: Mary Lund is a Principal Analyst Global Trade at Raytheon, based in the Dallas area, where she has worked remotely for approaching 9 years with 15+ years of deep ITAR/EAR technical expertise.

key_criteria.evidence: Lauren brings 14+ years of aerospace industry experience exclusively at Boeing, working across commercial airplane programs including the 737 and supporting fabrication divisions. She has worked on the 767-KC-46A military airplane program and maintains familiarity with ITAR requirements through commercial programs that cross over with military applications. She demonstrates industry-specific knowledge by asking informed questions about whether Doncasters supports specific military programs like

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Lauren currently supports Boeing's global operations across the Zhoushan China delivery center and fabrication sites in Canada, UK, and Australia, demonstrating comfort working with international stakeholders. She describes her organization as supporting "everyone and anybody within the company," indicating broad cross-functional engagement. Her previous procurement role required her to serve as an intermediary between highly technical engineers and suppliers, translating complex technical requi

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Lauren has 5-6 years of dedicated export compliance experience at Boeing and describes herself as "heavy on BIS licensing," with deep proficiency supporting raw materials teams for titanium and aluminum bar exports. She is "very familiar" with titanium export requirements, has completed index floor licensing, and demonstrates strong working knowledge of SnapR, AES, DDTC, and DECKS systems. However, her ITAR and DDTC licensing experience is admittedly limited—she has completed one DFT5 license an

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Lauren demonstrates working knowledge of the US export control regulatory environment including ITAR, EAR, DDTC, and related systems. She has worked under State Department consent agreements, collaborated with special compliance officers, and participated in auditing and procedure updates to move compliance programs "from gray areas to black-and-white procedures." She shows sophisticated understanding of how regulatory enforcement shifts with political administrations, noting the need to "figure

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Lauren currently operates as an individual contributor at Boeing, demonstrating comfort working independently without direct reports. She describes her current role as "hands-on" and expressed strong interest when the Doncasters role was described as not "maintain the status quo" but rather "being in the middle of things and having a real impact." Her comment that the role description "sounds a lot like my day job right now" suggests alignment with the hands-on operational nature. However, all o

compensation.expected_total: $150,000 - $175,000 total compensation; subsequently indicated flexibility to accept $140,000 - $150,000 base + 10% variable bonus
compensation.flexibility: Lauren initially stated expectation of $150-175k total comp but when informed this was higher than the target range of $140-150k base + 10% bonus, she confirmed "that would be fine by me," demonstrating meaningful flexibility and no compensation-related deal-breakers.

our_take.text: We believe Lauren presents as an articulate and well-spoken professional with solid BIS licensing expertise, though her ITAR experience is admittedly lighter—she has completed a DFT5 license but acknowledges this is not her primary strength compared to colleagues dedicated to military programs.

candidates.flash_summary: Lauren Sakahara is a Trade Control Specialist - Export at Boeing, based in the Seattle area, where she has spent 14 years 3 months with deep BIS licensing expertise supporting global manufacturing operations.

She explicitly handles DDTC licensing for defense contracts at Safran, managing submissions for missile technology and equipment that require specialized ATF licenses. She described handling 'everything from licenses and facilitating any paperwork or document certification needed for any export or import related issues.' At Sellmark Corporation, she 'acted as the Empowered Official for ITAR and EAR related act

key_criteria.evidence: The candidate demonstrates acute familiarity with the US export control regulatory environment. She confirmed direct experience with DDTC licensing processes, ITAR, and EAR across all three major roles. At Safran, she is 'responsible for functional adherence to all applicable trade compliance standards and regulations such as ITAR, EAR, FTR, OFAC and C-TPAT' and serves as Export Control Officer coordinating with Empowered Officials and Export Control Coordinators. She also possesses specialized

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: The candidate demonstrates strong comfort with hands-on, independent work in dynamic environments. She described her current role as 'very hands-on,' noting she works across all departments and manages 'different levels of chaos' accumulated over 15 years. Her response to the tariff crisis exemplifies entrepreneurial agility—she led a rapid scaling effort during what she called 'a whirlwind that just never stopped,' ultimately stabilizing operations. She expressed enthusiasm for Doncasters' entr

compensation.flexibility: Not discussed in detail. The candidate confirmed the $120K-140K range aligns with her expectations, and the recruiter confirmed this matches the role's salary range, indicating no apparent compensation barriers.

our_take.text: We believe this candidate presents a compelling profile with deep aerospace/defense compliance expertise and demonstrated ability to operate independently across geographically dispersed operations. Her enthusiasm for the entrepreneurial environment and comfort starting as a sole compliance function owner aligns well with Doncasters' hands-on culture, though we should validate her UK/Germany regulatory familiarity and CMMC/FAR-DFAR experience in follow-up discussions.

candidates.flash_summary: Veaseleti Lupeheke is currently Trade Compliance Manager at Safran Power Units, overseeing trade compliance across two US sites with 15+ years of aerospace/defense compliance expertise.

He works directly with internal business units and established programs "giving them kind of guidance and licensing support on a day-to-day basis" without direc

key_criteria.evidence: Niklas possesses deep US export compliance expertise, stating his current role involves "a lot of the licensing, it's a lot of ITAR, the EAR, it's a lot of the classification" plus "operational shipment type stuff looking at export documentation, EEI filings that are going outside of the US." He dedicates approximately 30% of his time to "overseeing the shipments themselves, looking at the export documentation, auditing the EEI filings, doing things like that to make sure we're all falling withi

He stated "everything's an acronym in defense" and confirmed current work is "a good chunk of my job, a lot of the licensing" involving ITAR/EAR classification and compliance. He works with CFR requirements, federalizations, EEI filings, and record-keeping obligations, stating he ensures "we're meeting kind of all those requirements and kind of record kee

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Niklas explicitly confirmed he operates in an entrepreneurial manner, stating "I would say so because it's kind of how you have to approach the role I'm in now." He described his manager's directive as "okay, so this is your site, this is your responsibility, there's no rigid structure on how you need to do anything, you can approach it as you see fit and just kind of make it work." He emphasized having "to adapt kind of that entrepreneurial mindset to integrating into the business, trying to ge

compensation.expected_total: Approximately $154,000 total package (10% increase above current $140,000)
compensation.flexibility: Niklas stated he is seeking "anything kind of 10% higher than that probably would be ideal" when referencing his current $140,000 total package, indicating this is a target rather than a rigid requirement. He did not express any deal-breakers or inflexibility on compensation structure.

our_take.text: We believe Niklas represents a technically strong candidate with the right regulatory expertise and entrepreneurial mindset, though his platform-specific defense background may require some adaptation to the secondary supplier precision manufacturing context at Doncasters.

our_take.text: We believe Michael represents an excellent fit for this role, bringing the precise blend of aerospace-focused US export compliance expertise, global stakeholder management, and M&A integration experience that Doncasters requires. His hands-on program-building background and current enterprise-scale responsibility position him well for both the brownfield US refinement and greenfield UK/Germany build components of this mandate.

At The Dupps Company she "fostered strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including customers, colleagues, brokers, and freight forwarders" and supported US-Brazil operations, though she clarified that Brazil had its own team handling import compliance and her scope remained US export controls. compensation.expected_total: Not explicitly stated; confirmed role would offer salary increase from current $135K total

our_take.text: We believe Vivian's entrepreneurial drive and program-building instincts are genuine strengths, but the international regulatory gap she herself identified is a fundamental mismatch for a role requiring UK/Germany program leadership from the outset. Her aerospace depth is too recent to provide the industry credibility Doncasters needs across global stakeholders.

candidates.flash_summary: Vivian Imamura-Baker is Lead Specialist - International Trade Compliance at GE Aerospace, with 8.5 years building trade compliance programs at The Dupps Company before joining GE.
